2605.22454.title: Don't Forget the Critic: Value-Based Data Rehearsal for Multi-Cyclic Continual Reinforcement Learning
2605.22454.paper_summary: Data rehearsal has emerged as a leading approach for mitigating catastrophic forgetting in Continual Reinforcement Learning (CRL). However, existing work remains confined to policy gradient frameworks, regularizing only actors due to the performance degradation incurred by critic regularization. This actor-centric approach overlooks the potential of data rehearsal for value function approximation. Moreover, existing evaluations in CRL rarely consider multi-cyclic environments where task sequences repeat, a critical real-world scenario that exacerbates forgetting and plasticity. We investigate data rehearsal for Deep Q-Networks using Q-value regularization in multi-cyclic settings and propose Qreg+NWLU which introduces two simple modifications: (1) continuous data rehearsal that dynamically collects and updates stored Q-values throughout training, and (2) "No-Wait" regularization that applies immediately rather than after the first task. Together, these modifications yield improvements in learning efficiency, forgetting mitigation, and knowledge transfer over Qreg and conventional CRL methods within value function approximation settings.
2605.22454.paper_summary_zh: 資料重演已成為減輕持續強化學習（CRL）中災難性遺忘的主要方法。然而，現有的研究仍然局限於策略梯度框架，只對演員進行正則化，因為評論員正則化會導致性能下降。這種以演員為中心的方法忽視了資料重演在價值函數近似中的潛力。此外，現有的CRL評估很少考慮多循環環境，其中任務序列重複，這是一個加劇遺忘和可塑性的關鍵現實場景。我們研究了在多循環環境中使用Q值正則化的深度Q網絡資料重演，並提出了Qreg+NWLU，該方法引入了兩個簡單的修改：（1）持續的資料重演，動態收集和更新訓練過程中的儲存Q值，以及（2）“無等待”正則化，立即應用而不是在第一個任務之後。這些修改共同提高了學習效率、減輕遺忘和知識轉移，相比於Qreg和傳統CRL方法在價值函數近似設置中的表現。
2605.22454.author: Benjamin Poole et.al.
2605.22454.authors: Benjamin Poole, Andrew Quinn, Li Yang, Minwoo Lee
